classification.description: Primary classification of the issue. `enhancement` matches the repo's GitHub label vocabulary — reporter-framed feature requests, missing-behavior asks, and scope-expansion proposals all land here.

claimed_version.description: Version string parsed from `--doctor` output, 'claude-desktop (X.Y.Z)' references, or AppImage filenames in the issue body. Null if no version is present. Drives the Stage 7 drift gate in later phases.

suggested_labels.description: Repo-vocabulary labels (e.g. 'priority: high', 'format: rpm', 'cowork', 'tray'). Stage 9 filters these through the cached repo label set and the blocklist before applying. Do not invent new labels.

duplicate_of.description: Issue number this duplicates, or null. Only set when classification is 'duplicate'.

regression_of.description: Set iff the reporter explicitly names a culprit PR or commit (e.g. 'broken since #305', 'after commit abc123'). Integer PR number for PR references; null for commit SHAs or when the reporter has not bisected.

policy_bucket.description: Repo-policy pattern the issue matches, independent of classification. 'none' when the issue is inside this project's packaging surface. Non-none buckets let the pipeline post a policy-grounded routing comment instead of the generic no-findings deferral. See the policy digest in the classify prompt.
